title: Excoecaria marginata
taxonomy: Excoecaria marginata is classified under the family Euphorbiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. This family consists of over 300 genera and 7,000 species, many of which are known for their unique and often poisonous compounds. Excoecaria marginata is a tropical plant native to the coastal regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. It belongs to the genus Excoecaria, which consists of about 10 species of trees and shrubs. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Excoecaria marginata is closely related to other plants such as the poinsettia and the castor oil plant.

intro: Excoecaria marginata, also known as the Yellow Mangrove or Mangrove of Death, is a tropical plant species native to the coastal regions of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Excoecaria marginata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antivenom properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
